steps.title: Spot the chronological signal
steps.content: The passage anchors two events to specific years—1891 and 1896—establishing a clear time gap. The second event (the film) occurred years after the first (the dance).

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Later,” directly signals temporal progression—showing that the film came after the performance. “However,” signals contrast, “In conclusion,” signals summary, and “In other words,” signals rephrasing; these don't fit the chronological relationship. That matches choice C — C is correct.
